flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b (Stage 0.5 reject fast-path) — reviewer's novelty verdict is reject and explicitly states 'this is not a local revise-to-field situation': the locked kernel has collapsed to a generic Manski/Balke-Pearl finite-LP argument plus a negative refutation of the prior rank-one zonotope conjecture, which is subfield-tier and below the field novelty target. Correctness defect (Theta_I(P) vs Theta_LP(P) mismatch in thm:lp-sharp) is repairable, but even after repair the surviving positive content is generic LP sharpness, so a Stage 0 re-derivation on the same conjecture would burn budget without lifting tier.

No localized Stage 0 patch can lift this to field tier — the angle itself is unsalvageable as conjectured.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of natural direct and indirect effects in mediation with unobserved post-treatment confounders, using negative-control proxies for the latent mediator-outcome confounder and a single binary treatment
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains empty for this kernel: ThmSmith/doc/API.md §3 reports no ExactID/PartialID mediation proxy-null proposal, and no in-catalogue result states a shared-null PNDE/TNIE joint identified set. The v5 patch preserves that repo-axis gap while replacing the outcome convention with a bounded mean-kernel polytope.","published_axis":"Closest published work remains DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 on proximal mediation point identification, ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 on nonunique bridges with invariant targets, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 on proxy partial identification for causal effects, and GabrielSachsSjolander2023 on nonsharp combination of decomposition bounds. None gives a sharp shared proxy-null line segment for the joint PNDE/TNIE identified set; v5 clarifies that the sharpness claim is a mean-kernel projection for bounded Y, not a binary-outcome response-type claim."}
seed_list: Prior work DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 would predict that proximal mediation needs bridge uniqueness for natural-effect point identification. Our seed conjectures that bridge rank-one failure still point-identifies PNDE or TNIE exactly when that effect's cross-world loading annihilates the proxy-null direction, because the same nonunique bridge enters different natural-effect mixtures.

Prior work DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 would imply that nonunique bridge equations are a nuisance failure mode. Our seed conjectures that the nonuniqueness is itself a sharp partial-ID coordinate whose interval endpoints are observable affine images, because binary proxy rank deficiency leaves a one-dimensional bridge fiber.

Prior work MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 would imply that double-negative-control rank failure blocks bridge correction. Our seed conjectures that mediation contrasts can survive ACE bridge failure, because mediator-distribution weights can cancel the bridge-null direction for one natural effect but not the other.

Prior work MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 establishes no-proxy partial-ID bounds for natural effects under relaxed mediation assumptions. Our seed conjectures that rank-deficient negative-control proxies strictly tighten only the natural effect whose endpoint response types are excluded by the proxy-null equation, because proxy information acts on bridge fibers rather than on cross-world assumptions directly.

Prior work GabrielSachsSjolander2023 would warn that separately sharp decomposition bounds need not combine sharply. Our seed conjectures that PNDE and TNIE proxy-null intervals share a common latent null coordinate and therefore have a non-rectangular joint sharp set, because both effects are affine functions of the same bridge fiber.

Prior work DingVanderWeele2016 bounds mediation effects through sensitivity parameters for unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. Our seed conjectures that a weak negative-control pair converts part of that sensitivity region into an observable proxy-null interval with sign-dependent collapse, because proxy rank determines which confounding directions remain feasible.

Prior work PenningDeVriesGroenwold2023 emphasizes caveats for weak or redundant negative controls. Our seed conjectures that redundancy is not all-or-nothing: a collinear proxy pair can still remove the no-proxy endpoint types for PNDE while leaving TNIE Manski-wide, because redundancy is target-loading-specific.

Prior work HanLuoZhangGu2025 studies partially observed mediator-endpoint confounders and would suggest measured confounder components are the tightening device. Our seed conjectures that binary proxies can produce the same or stricter endpoint exclusion without observing any component of U, because proxy bridge equations constrain latent response mixtures.

Prior work CaiKurokiPearlTian2008 gives direct-effect bounds with a confounded intermediate variable. Our seed conjectures that natural direct effects have a new proxy-null sign-switch absent from controlled direct effects, because the mediator's natural distribution weights the bridge-null direction differently across treatment arms.

Prior work SunYe2021 identifies mediation effects via heteroskedasticity under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. Our seed conjectures that when heteroskedastic point ID is unavailable, proxy-rank geometry yields a sharp finite bound with zero-width subfaces, because the bridge fiber can be projected directly onto natural effects.

Prior work AvinShpitserPearl2005 shows path-specific effects can be unidentified under intermediate confounding. Our seed conjectures a positive partial-ID boundary: even in that nonidentified regime, proxy-null orthogonality can certify zero-width natural-effect intervals, because negative controls add equations absent from the graphical criterion.

Prior work ImaiKeeleYamamoto2010 frames mediator-outcome confounding through sensitivity analysis. Our seed conjectures that finite binary negative controls replace an analyst-chosen sensitivity parameter by an observable bridge-null coefficient, because the proxy law itself determines the remaining confounding direction.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 would predict that proximal mediation needs bridge uniqueness for natural-effect point identification. Our seed conjectures that bridge rank-one failure still point-identifies PNDE or TNIE exactly when that effect's cross-world loading annihilates the proxy-null direction, because the same nonunique bridge enters different natural-effect mixtures.

anchor_paper.author: Dukes, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Proximal mediation identifies natural direct and indirect effects using proxy bridge conditions and develops efficient estimators.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Completeness weakening, not blocked: the proposed phenomenon is target-specific point-ID/partial-ID phase transition under rank failure, not a broader hypothesis class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 because bridge nonuniqueness just means the functional is unidentified.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their positive theorem assumes bridge uniqueness; it does not classify when the natural-effect functional is invariant over a nonunique bridge fiber.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 would imply that nonunique bridge equations are a nuisance failure mode. Our seed conjectures that the nonuniqueness is itself a sharp partial-ID coordinate whose interval endpoints are observable affine images, because binary proxy rank deficiency leaves a one-dimensional bridge fiber.

anchor_paper.author: Dukes, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Proxy bridge functions point-identify natural effects under proximal completeness/bridge uniqueness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens completeness, not blocked: the surprise is closed-form sharp affine geometry of the failure boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate finite-dimensional linear algebra once the bridge matrix is singular.
seed_details.rebuttal: Linear algebra gives a fiber; the nontrivial gap is sharp causal realizability and natural-effect endpoint coupling over that fiber.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 would imply that double-negative-control rank failure blocks bridge correction. Our seed conjectures that mediation contrasts can survive ACE bridge failure, because mediator-distribution weights can cancel the bridge-null direction for one natural effect but not the other.

anchor_paper.author: Miao, Shi, Li, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: A double-negative-control confounding bridge identifies ACE under bridge and negative-control conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: switching from ACE to natural effects reveals a cancellation/identification gap hidden in ACE.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from general linear-functional invariance over a nullspace.
seed_details.rebuttal: The mediation-specific loadings are cross-world mediator distributions; the closest ACE bridge papers do not derive or interpret this target-specific cancellation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 establishes no-proxy partial-ID bounds for natural effects under relaxed mediation assumptions. Our seed conjectures that rank-deficient negative-control proxies strictly tighten only the natural effect whose endpoint response types are excluded by the proxy-null equation, because proxy information acts on bridge fibers rather than on cross-world assumptions directly.

anchor_paper.author: Miles, Kanki, Meloni, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Partial-identification bounds for natural effects are available when key mediation assumptions are relaxed.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method with new term, not blocked: proxies can tighten one natural effect but not the other, a target-specific endpoint exclusion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already contained in no-proxy bounds plus adding linear constraints.
seed_details.rebuttal: Generic constraint addition does not give the proposed iff criterion for strict tightening or the PNDE/TNIE asymmetry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GabrielSachsSjolander2023 would warn that separately sharp decomposition bounds need not combine sharply. Our seed conjectures that PNDE and TNIE proxy-null intervals share a common latent null coordinate and therefore have a non-rectangular joint sharp set, because both effects are affine functions of the same bridge fiber.

anchor_paper.author: Gabriel, Sachs, and Sjolander
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp bounds for decomposition effects with multiple binary mediators cannot generally be obtained by adding/subtracting separate component bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: identifies shared proxy-null coordinate causing joint nonrectangularity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a generic warning from GabrielSachsSjolander2023 about componentwise bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their warning is about multi-mediator decomposition terms; this seed isolates a one-mediator negative-control bridge fiber and proposes its exact joint geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DingVanderWeele2016 bounds mediation effects through sensitivity parameters for unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. Our seed conjectures that a weak negative-control pair converts part of that sensitivity region into an observable proxy-null interval with sign-dependent collapse, because proxy rank determines which confounding directions remain feasible.

anchor_paper.author: Ding and VanderWeele
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp sensitivity bounds can be derived for direct and indirect effects without parametric assumptions about unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Orthogonal to blocklist: replaces analyst-chosen sensitivity restrictions by observed proxy equations and predicts sign-dependent collapse.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just sensitivity analysis with one parameter estimated from proxies.
seed_details.rebuttal: The bridge-null interval is not a scalar sensitivity parameter; it is a feasibility projection from observed proxy laws with target-specific loadings.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work PenningDeVriesGroenwold2023 emphasizes caveats for weak or redundant negative controls. Our seed conjectures that redundancy is not all-or-nothing: a collinear proxy pair can still remove the no-proxy endpoint types for PNDE while leaving TNIE Manski-wide, because redundancy is target-loading-specific.

anchor_paper.author: Penning de Vries and Groenwold
anchor_paper.claim: Negative controls can detect and correct unmeasured confounding but require careful assumptions and have important caveats.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; converts a caveat into a target-specific theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an applied caution, not a theorem-level gap.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed turns the caveat into a finite sharpness classification for redundant binary proxies.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HanLuoZhangGu2025 studies partially observed mediator-endpoint confounders and would suggest measured confounder components are the tightening device. Our seed conjectures that binary proxies can produce the same or stricter endpoint exclusion without observing any component of U, because proxy bridge equations constrain latent response mixtures.

anchor_paper.author: Han, Luo, Zhang, and Gu
anchor_paper.claim: Bounds and identification for CDE, NDE, and NIE are derived when mediator-endpoint confounders are partially observed.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment/measurement variation, not blocked: proxy equations can dominate observed-component bounds through endpoint exclusion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just replacing observed confounders with proxies, a standard proximal move.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed comparison is not point identification; it is sharp endpoint dominance in rank-deficient proxy bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CaiKurokiPearlTian2008 gives direct-effect bounds with a confounded intermediate variable. Our seed conjectures that natural direct effects have a new proxy-null sign-switch absent from controlled direct effects, because the mediator's natural distribution weights the bridge-null direction differently across treatment arms.

anchor_paper.author: Cai, Kuroki, Pearl, and Tian
anchor_paper.claim: Symbolic Balke-Pearl linear programming gives closed-form ACDE bounds with confounded intermediate variables.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: natural effects expose cross-world mediator weighting absent from CDE.
seed_details.referee_objection: Natural effects are standard weighted controlled direct effects, so bounds follow by weighting CDE bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: GabrielSachsSjolander2023 warns componentwise combination can be nonsharp; the proxy-null coordinate couples the weights before projection.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SunYe2021 identifies mediation effects via heteroskedasticity under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. Our seed conjectures that when heteroskedastic point ID is unavailable, proxy-rank geometry yields a sharp finite bound with zero-width subfaces, because the bridge fiber can be projected directly onto natural effects.

anchor_paper.author: Sun and Ye
anchor_paper.claim: Natural direct and indirect effects can be identified using heteroskedasticity restrictions in the observed data law.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; compares two different point-ID devices through a partial-ID boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just another identification strategy, not a contradiction or gap.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for sharp fallback bounds when the heteroskedastic restriction is not imposed, using observed proxies instead.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AvinShpitserPearl2005 shows path-specific effects can be unidentified under intermediate confounding. Our seed conjectures a positive partial-ID boundary: even in that nonidentified regime, proxy-null orthogonality can certify zero-width natural-effect intervals, because negative controls add equations absent from the graphical criterion.

anchor_paper.author: Avin, Shpitser, and Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Path-specific effects have graphical identification limits, especially under intermediate confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: introduces proxy equations that change zero-width identified-set status.
seed_details.referee_objection: This conflicts with graphical nonidentifiability results.
seed_details.rebuttal: The graphical result lacks negative-control proxy restrictions; proxy equations are additional observed-law constraints, so no conflict is implied.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ImaiKeeleYamamoto2010 frames mediator-outcome confounding through sensitivity analysis. Our seed conjectures that finite binary negative controls replace an analyst-chosen sensitivity parameter by an observable bridge-null coefficient, because the proxy law itself determines the remaining confounding direction.

anchor_paper.author: Imai, Keele, and Yamamoto
anchor_paper.claim: ACME/NDE identification follows under sequential ignorability, with sensitivity analysis for violations.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; reframes sensitivity as an observed proxy-null projection.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already the motivation for proximal causal inference.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proximal literature gives point-ID under completeness; this seed targets the sharp partial-ID geometry when completeness fails.

proposed_from.literature_map: DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 (Biometrika, doi:10.1093/biomet/asad015, https://biblio.ugent.be/publication/01HRSH6RQ7T6SE01X7R20RY0G8) gives proximal identification of natural direct/indirect effects using proxy variables; open boundary is failed completeness. MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 (Statistical Theory and Related Fields/arXiv:1808.04945, https://arxiv.org/abs/1808.04945) develops double-negative-control bridge inference for ACE; tension is rank failure versus partial ID. PenningDeVriesGroenwold2023 (Stat Methods Med Res, doi:10.1177/09622802231181230) reviews negative-control correction and caveats; phenomenon is weak/redundant controls. DingVanderWeele2016 (Biometrika, doi:10.1093/biomet/asw012, https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/103/2/483/1744249) gives sharp sensitivity bounds for mediation under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding. MilesKankiMeloniTchetgen2017 (JCI/arXiv:1509.01652, https://arxiv.org/abs/1509.01652) gives partial-ID bounds for natural effects when cross-world/exposure-induced assumptions fail. GabrielSachsSjolander2023 (JASA, doi:10.1080/01621459.2022.2057316) shows decomposition-effect sharp bounds cannot usually be combined componentwise; relevant to joint PNDE/TNIE coupling. CaiKurokiPearlTian2008 (Biometrics/PubMed 18162106, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18162106/) gives Balke-Pearl-style direct-effect bounds with confounded intermediate variables. HanLuoZhangGu2025 (Journal of Multivariate Analysis, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0047259X25001605) studies bounds when mediator-endpoint confounders are partially observed; contrast is proxies rather than observed confounder components. ImaiKeeleYamamoto2010 (Statistical Science/arXiv:1011.1079, https://arxiv.org/abs/1011.1079) anchors sequential-ignorability mediation and sensitivity analysis. TchetgenShpitser2012 (Annals of Statistics, doi:10.1214/12-AOS990) anchors semiparametric natural-effect theory. SunYe2021 (arXiv:2012.06762, https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.06762) point-identifies natural effects via heteroskedasticity rather than proxies. AvinShpitserPearl2005 and Pearl2001 supply the path-specific/natural-effect identification boundary.
